title: kube down/play --replace: handle absent objects
body:
Make sure that `kube down` and `kube play --replace` do not error out when an object does not exist (or has already been removed).  Such kind of teardown should not be treated as an ordinary `rm` but as an `rm --ignore`.  It's purpose it to make sure that all objects in a YAML are removed; even if they existed only partially.

Fixes: #19711

a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.

@ygalblum @umohnani8 PTAL

This is a behavioral change but I strongly believe that `kube down` should not error out when a given object does not exist and just remove those that exist.

> I wonder if we should change the default behavior or add a new `--ignore` flag

I don´t think an `--ignore` flag is needed as I believe a teardown's purpose is to make sure everything's cleaned up and can run again. It already ignored non-existent containers but stopped after that half way.
